steps.title: Use the contrast goal
steps.content: The student wants to contrast the purposes of the two maps in The Hobbit. A successful sentence must identify what the opening map does, identify what the closing map does, and make the difference between those purposes clear.

steps.title: Rule out incomplete contrasts
steps.content: Choice B only identifies the maps and publication details. Choice C says both maps have purposes but does not state them. Choice D mentions the author, year, and maps without contrasting their purposes. The remaining choice contrasts the opening map's introduction to the fictional world with the closing map's help in reconstructing the story. The answer is A.
